indication: Non-metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (nmCRPC): proven histological/cytological adenocarcinoma of prostate without neuroendocrine differentiation or small cell features; non-metastatic disease; hormone-resistant (castrate-resistant) disease with 3 rising PSA levels; serum testosterone <1.7nmol/L; PSA >=2ng/ml; PSA doubling time <=10 months; no prior 2nd generation AR inhibitors or CYP17 inhibitors; given in combination with androgen deprivation therapy; WHO PS 0, 1 or 2

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: 600mg twice daily (days 1-28 inclusive). Available as 300mg film-coated tablets. Swallow whole with food and a glass of water. 12 cycles will be set in Aria; continue until disease progression.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day

Formal medical review by start of third 4-weekly cycle
special_warnings: Androgen receptor inhibitor - oral agent
Adverse effects include: fatigue/asthenia, decreased neutrophil count, raised bilirubin/AST, ischaemic heart disease, heart failure, rash, musculoskeletal pain, pain in extremities, fractures
Use of strong CYP3A4 and P-gp inducers may decrease plasma concentration - not recommended unless no therapeutic alternative
Co-administration with rosuvastatin should be avoided unless no therapeutic alternative
Darolutamide may increase plasma concentrations of BCRP, OATP1B1 and OATP1B3 substrates (e.g. methotrexate, sulfasalazine, fluvastatin, atorvastatin, pitavastatin) - monitor for adverse reactions
Treatment break >6 weeks beyond expected 4-week cycle length requires approval form
Treatment should be supervised by a consultant oncologist
Given only in combination with androgen deprivation therapy
